name: AWS Cognito API
what_it_does: AWS Cognito REST and SDK API — managed user directory and authentication service providing user pools (signup/signin/MFA) and identity pools (federated identity for AWS resource access), enabling agents to manage user accounts, authentication flows, and AWS federated access at scale.
use_cases: Agents managing user account lifecycle — creating, confirming, updating, and disabling users in Cognito user pools via admin API
Token management — agents using Cognito to issue JWT tokens for application authentication and validating tokens in API gateways
MFA administration — agents enabling/disabling MFA for specific users and managing TOTP device associations
Group management — agents assigning users to Cognito groups for role-based access control in downstream services
Custom auth flows — agents implementing custom challenge responses (CAPTCHA, risk-based auth) via Cognito Lambda triggers
not_for: B2B enterprise SSO — Cognito handles consumer identity well but is limited for complex enterprise SSO; use Okta or Auth0 for enterprise SAML/OIDC federation
Non-AWS architectures — Cognito is tightly coupled to AWS ecosystem; use Auth0 or Firebase Auth for cloud-agnostic auth
Simple projects — Cognito setup complexity is high for small applications; use Supabase Auth or Firebase Auth for simpler use cases
best_when: You're building an AWS-native application and need managed user authentication with tight integration with API Gateway, Lambda authorizers, and IAM roles.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SSO, advanced B2B features, or want to avoid AWS vendor lock-in — Auth0 or Okta are more flexible for complex identity scenarios.

auth.notes: Admin API requires AWS IAM credentials (SigV4 signing) with appropriate IAM policies. User-facing operations (InitiateAuth, etc.) use Client ID + optional Client Secret — no IAM required. OAuth2/OIDC token endpoints for JWT issuance. Lambda triggers for custom auth flows. No traditional API key — AWS access key/secret key via SDK.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50,000 monthly active users free for user pools; always free for first 50,000 MAU
pricing.paid_tiers: User pools: $0.0055 per MAU beyond 50K; Advanced security: additional cost; Identity pools: $0 for most use cases
pricing.notes: Very affordable for moderate scale. Advanced security features (anomaly detection, compromised credential check) cost extra. Identity pools are effectively free. Pricing per monthly active user, not per API call.

performance.rate_limits: AdminCreateUser: 50 req/s; AdminGetUser: 120 req/s; InitiateAuth: 100 req/s (per user pool); limits vary by operation

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS-standard API with detailed documentation. Error messages include error code and message. Some Cognito-specific errors (NotAuthorizedException, UserNotFoundException) require specific handling. SigV4 signing is automatic via AWS SDK.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: AdminCreateUser is idempotent if user already exists (returns existing user). AdminUpdateUserAttributes is idempotent. Token operations are non-idempotent — new tokens issued each call.
known_agent_gotchas: AdminCreateUser requires IAM permissions — agents using user-pool-level operations need separate IAM policy from app-level operations
Per-user-pool rate limits are separate from global account limits — agents managing multiple user pools must track limits per pool
Temporary password from AdminCreateUser requires user to change on first login — agents initiating flows should handle FORCE_CHANGE_PASSWORD challenge
Token validation requires fetching JWKS from Cognito endpoint — agents should cache the JWKS and refresh periodically, not on every request
User pool ID format (region_poolId) must match region — agents with multi-region deployments must route to correct regional endpoint

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS IAM with fine-grained policies. HIPAA BAA, FedRAMP authorized, PCI-DSS, SOC2 Type II. Advanced security features include compromised credential detection and adaptive authentication. GDPR compliant with EU data residency.
